steps.title: Identify the structure before the blank
steps.content: The sentence opens with a series of appositive noun phrases describing a literary work: "A coming-of-age story, a social meditation, and a sober gaze into the dark maw of French colonialism." Whatever fills the blank must be the subject those appositives rename, and it must control the verb "interrogates."

steps.title: Apply the modifier/subject rule
steps.content: The subject right after the comma series must be a literary work (the novel). Only an option whose subject is the novel both satisfies the appositive series and supplies a singular subject for "interrogates."

steps.title: Per-choice elimination
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Morocco gained its independence ... which': makes "Morocco" the subject the appositives rename, which is illogical; Morocco is not a coming-of-age story. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Chraïbi's debut novel, published two years before Morocco gained its independence,': gives "novel" as the subject the appositives rename and as the subject of "interrogates." ✓
  • Choice C — 'Chraïbi wrote a debut novel that ...': makes "Chraïbi" the subject the appositives rename, so the series describes the author, not the book. ✗
  • Choice D — 'published two years before ... Chraïbi wrote a debut novel that': the opening participial phrase "published ..." dangles — it should modify the novel, but it is immediately followed by "Chraïbi," creating a misplaced modifier. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
